title: Introduction to Computing
summary: Traces the evolution of computing from the abacus to the von Neumann model, defines universal computing machines, and outlines a brief history of key contributors and milestones.
content_points: Operations that can be performed evolved from addition (abacus) to branching (Babbage) to the memorized-program model (von Neumann).
A universal computing machine can behave like any particular machine, solving any problem solvable by a specific machine.
Key historical figures: Boole (1854), Gödel (1935), Shannon (1938), von Neumann (1946), Shockley et al. (1947).
Integrated circuits followed the transistor, enabling today's computing.
learning_objectives: Describe the evolution of computing devices
Identify key historical contributors to computer science
